title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the delicious Korean seafood pancake, at home! This easy recipe brings the taste of Korea to your kitchen. Get tips for a crispy texture.
seo.h1: Haemul Pajeon: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Delicious Haemul Pajeon, a crispy Korean seafood pancake with green onions and various seafood.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a homemade Haemul Pajeon, a savory and crispy Korean seafood pancake, perfect as an appetizer or light meal.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved savory Korean pancake, generously studded with a variety of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Its crispy edges and tender interior make it an irresistible appetizer or light meal, often enjoyed with a tangy dipping sauce. This classic dish offers a delightful blend of flavors and textures, perfect for sharing.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, clams, cut into bite-sized pieces
about 8-10 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch lengths

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and prepare your assorted seafood, cutting larger pieces like squid into bite-sized portions. Pat them dry thoroughly. Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and red chili if using.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, and cold water until just combined. A few lumps are fine; do not overmix. Stir in the lightly beaten egg. The batter should be slightly thicker than crepe batter.

instructions.title: Prepare Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and toasted sesame seeds. Add gochugaru if you prefer a little heat. Set aside.

instructions.title: Heat Pan and Start First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add about 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil. Once hot, pour about half of the batter into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin, round pancake.

instructions.title: Add Toppings
instructions.text: Quickly arrange half of the prepared seafood and half of the green onion stalks evenly over the wet batter. Add half of the sliced onion and red chili if using. Press the toppings gently into the batter.

instructions.title: Cook Until Golden
instructions.text: Cook for about 5-7 minutes until the bottom of the pancake is golden brown and crispy, and the edges appear set. You may need to add a little more oil around the edges to help with crisping.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side until it is also golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. Press down lightly with the spatula to ensure even cooking and crispiness.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and cut into wedges. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce. Repeat the process for the second pancake.

tips: Use very cold water for the batter to inhibit gluten development, resulting in a crispier pancake. You can even add an ice cube to the batter.
Do not overmix the batter; a few lumps are perfectly fine and can contribute to a lighter texture. Overmixing can make the pancake tough.

Ensure your pan is very hot before adding the batter and sufficient oil is used. This creates that desirable crispy exterior. Adding a bit more oil around the edges during cooking can enhance crispiness.
article:
Korean cuisine is a world of vibrant flavors, fresh ingredients, and communal dining experiences. Among its countless treasures, one dish stands out for its irresistible crunch, savory filling, and comforting appeal: Haemul Pajeon, the beloved Korean Seafood Pancake. More than just a simple pancake, Haemul Pajeon is a culinary masterpiece, a symphony of textures and tastes that has captivated food lovers worldwide. Whether you’re a seasoned fan of Korean food or a curious newcomer, learning about and making this iconic dish is a delightful journey. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to create the perfect Haemul Pajeon right in your own kitchen.

## What is Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake?

Haemul Pajeon (해물파전) literally translates to "seafood scallion pancake," and true to its name, it’s a savory Korean pancake generously studded with various seafood and an abundance of fresh scallions. Unlike the sweet, fluffy pancakes you might enjoy for breakfast, Haemul Pajeon is a savory appetizer or a hearty snack, often enjoyed with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or soju. Its origins trace back to coastal regions where fresh seafood was plentiful, evolving into a staple across the country.

The hallmark of a great Haemul Pajeon is its contrast: a wonderfully crispy exterior that gives way to a soft, chewy interior packed with tender seafood, crisp scallions, and sometimes other vegetables like carrots or onions. The batter, typically made from a mix of wheat flour, rice flour, and water (or sometimes broth), provides a delicate base that allows the fresh flavors of the seafood and greens to shine. It’s a dish that’s both satisfyingly rich and surprisingly refreshing, making it a popular choice in Korean restaurants and home kitchens alike.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting authentic Haemul Pajeon starts with gathering the right ingredients. Freshness is key, especially when it comes to the seafood.

**For the Batter:**
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   ½ cup rice flour (for extra crispiness, optional but recommended)
*   1 teaspoon sea salt
*   ½ teaspoon black pepper
*   1 ½ cups cold water or anchovy broth (broth adds depth of flavor)
*   1 large egg

**For the Filling:**
*   1 lb mixed seafood, thawed if frozen (e.g., shrimp, calamari rings, mussels, bay scallops). Cut larger pieces into bite-sized chunks.
*   1 large bunch of green onions (scallions), about 10-15 stalks, washed and trimmed. Cut into 2-3 inch lengths.
*   ½ medium onion, thinly sliced
*   1 red chili pepper, thinly sliced (optional, for a touch of heat and color)
*   2-3 tablespoons vegetable oil (or any neutral cooking oil) for frying

**For the Dipping Sauce:**
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   ½ teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional)
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)
*   1 teaspoon finely chopped green onion

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Haemul Pajeon is an enjoyable process, and with these steps, you’ll be flipping perfect pancakes in no time.

1.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce:** In a small bowl, combine all dipping sauce ingredients. Stir well and set aside to allow the flavors to meld.
2.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables:** If using frozen seafood, ensure it's fully thawed and patted dry. Cut any large pieces into uniform, bite-sized portions. Wash and trim the green onions, then cut them into 2-3 inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and red chili (if using).
3.  **Make the Batter:** In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, and pepper. Gradually add the cold water (or broth) while continuously whisking to prevent lumps. The batter should be smooth and slightly thinner than pancake batter – it should have a consistency that coats the back of a spoon but drips off easily. Whisk in the egg until fully incorporated.
4.  **Combine Filling with Batter (Optional Method):** Some prefer to mix the seafood and vegetables directly into the batter. For a more traditional restaurant-style Haemul Pajeon, you'll arrange them in the pan first. For simplicity, you can gently fold the prepared seafood, green onions, sliced onion, and red chili (if using) into the batter. Be careful not to overmix.
5.  **Heat the Pan:** Place a large non-stick skillet or a cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the entire bottom of the pan. Let the oil get hot – it should shimmer slightly.
6.  **Cook the Pancake:**
    *   **Method A (Restaurant Style):** Ladle a thin layer of batter into the hot pan, spreading it slightly. Immediately arrange a generous amount of green onion lengths, seafood pieces, and sliced onion evenly over the batter. Then, ladle a bit more batter over the toppings, just enough to bind them. This method gives a beautiful, organized presentation.
    *   **Method B (Mixed Batter):** Pour about 1 to 1 ½ cups of the combined batter and filling mixture into the hot pan, spreading it out to form a large, round pancake, about ½ to ¾ inch thick. Don't overcrowd the pan; you might need to cook in batches.
7.  **Fry until Golden and Crispy:** Cook for 4-6 minutes on the first side, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges appear set. You can carefully lift an edge with a spatula to check.
8.  **Flip the Pancake:** This is the trickiest part! Carefully slide a large, sturdy spatula (or two spatulas) underneath the pancake. With a swift, confident motion, flip the Haemul Pajeon over. If it's too large, you can cut it in half in the pan before flipping.
9.  **Cook the Second Side:** Add a little more oil around the edges of the pan if needed. Press down gently on the pancake with your spatula to ensure even cooking and crispiness. Cook for another 4-5 minutes, or until the second side is golden brown and the seafood is cooked through.
10. **Serve Hot:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Slice it into wedges or squares and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, crispy yet tender Haemul Pajeon requires a few insider tricks:

*   **Cold Batter:** Using very cold water (or broth) for your batter helps create a crispier pancake by inhibiting gluten development and making the starch gelatinize more effectively upon contact with hot oil.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil:** Haemul Pajeon is meant to be pan-fried, not just lightly cooked. Generous oil is crucial for achieving that desirable golden-brown, crispy crust. Don't be afraid to add more around the edges as it cooks.
*   **High Heat (Initially):** Start with medium-high heat to get that initial sizzle and crisp, then you might slightly reduce it to medium to ensure the interior cooks through without burning the exterior.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from the seafood can lead to a soggy pancake. Pat all seafood thoroughly dry with paper towels before adding it to the batter or arranging it in the pan.
*   **Rice Flour is Your Friend:** While optional, adding rice flour to your batter significantly contributes to a lighter, crispier texture. Don't skip it if you want that authentic crunch.
*   **Even Distribution:** Whether you mix the filling into the batter or arrange it in the pan, ensure the seafood and scallions are evenly distributed throughout the Haemul Pajeon for consistent flavor and texture in every bite.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Like most pancakes, Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan when it’s at its crispiest and hottest.

## Serving Suggestions

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways:

*   **As an Appetizer:** It makes a fantastic starter for any Korean meal, setting the stage with its rich flavors.
*   **With Drinks:** Traditionally, Haemul Pajeon is a popular "anju" (drinking snack) paired with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or soju. The savory, slightly greasy pancake beautifully complements the refreshing, milky beverage.
*   **Light Meal:** A large Haemul Pajeon can be a satisfying light lunch or dinner, especially when paired with a simple side of kimchi and perhaps a fresh green salad.
*   **Side Dish:** It can also be served as a side dish alongside other Korean staples like bibimbap, bulgogi, or stews like sundubu jjigae.

Always serve your Haemul Pajeon hot, cut into wedges or squares, with the accompanying soy-vinegar dipping sauce on the side.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment with variations to suit your taste or what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon (Yachaejeon):** Omit the seafood entirely and increase the amount of green onions and other vegetables. Sliced mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), carrots, zucchini, or bell peppers work wonderfully.
*   **Meat Pajeon (Gogi Pajeon):** Replace some or all of the seafood with thinly sliced beef (bulgogi style) or pork. Ensure the meat is cut very thin so it cooks quickly.
*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** Add about ½ cup of chopped, well-squeezed kimchi to the batter for a tangy, spicy kick. This is a very popular variation.
*   **Different Seafood:** Don't limit yourself to the suggested seafood mix. Feel free to use only shrimp, only calamari, or add oysters, clams, or even small pieces of white fish.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili pepper in the pancake and gochugaru in the dipping sauce to control the heat. For more spice, you can also add a pinch of gochujang (Korean chili paste) to the batter.
*   **Batter Enhancements:** For an even richer flavor, some recipes use a bit of dashi (Japanese fish stock) or vegetable broth instead of water in the batter. A tiny pinch of baking powder can also be added for a slightly fluffier texture, though it might compromise crispiness.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a recipe; it's an experience. The joy of creating this crispy, savory Korean seafood pancake from scratch is incredibly rewarding, and the resulting flavors are simply unforgettable. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your skillet, and embark on a delicious culinary adventure that will transport your taste buds straight to the bustling streets of Seoul. Enjoy the satisfying crunch and the burst of fresh seafood and scallion in every bite!
